patch.value: MCP server: request-scoped extension registries (process-global set/clear can race under concurrent native-action requests)

runDynamicExtensionAction sets process-global active extension registries (setActiveExtensionHooks/Commands/Parsers/...) then awaits the handler, and clears them in finally. The stdio MCP server processes native-action requests sequentially today, so the per-request set+clear is safe. But the process-global pattern is pre-existing and would race if requests are ever processed concurrently (a newer request overwrites globals; a completing request's finally clears them). Follow-up: scope the active registries per-request (pass them through the call instead of mutating module-global state) so the MCP server is concurrency-safe. Surfaced by Gemini on PR #150 (server.ts:668); deferred as a separate, larger refactor (the SDK PR only added the teardown finally).

value.author: mcp-agent
value.text: Starting work: making MCP native-action registry handling concurrency-safe (set/clear of process-global extension registries in runDynamicExtensionAction). Evaluating async-mutex serialization vs request-scoped registries.

value.author: mcp-agent
value.text: DESIGN DECISION: chose serialization (option 1) over request-scoped registries. runDynamicExtensionAction's critical section (set process-global registries -> run handler -> clear in finally) is now wrapped in a dedicated SerialQueue (reusing src/core/shared/serial-queue.ts, the same audited FIFO mutex the stdio transport already uses for line ordering). Trade-off: request-scoping would require plumbing per-request registries through every runActive* consumer (runActiveCommandHandler, on-write/on-read/on-index hooks, parser/preflight/renderer overrides) across main.ts, create.ts and deep item-store call sites that read the module-global lazily mid-handler — a large refactor for zero behavioral win today, since the stdio transport handles requests sequentially anyway. The lock covers the FULL activation cycle (load -> activate -> set -> run -> clear -> deactivate), exactly preserving today's sequential semantics for direct handleRequest callers (tests, future concurrent transports). Full per-request registry plumbing remains possible later if true intra-server concurrency is ever wanted.

value.author: mcp-agent
value.text: DONE. Fix: src/mcp/server.ts — added module-level dynamicExtensionActionQueue (createSerialQueue from src/core/shared/serial-queue.ts) and split runDynamicExtensionAction so cheap guards (noExtensions, settings-exists) stay outside the lock while the entire activation cycle (readSettings -> loadExtensions -> activateExtensions -> set globals -> run handler -> clear globals -> deactivateExtensions) runs in runDynamicExtensionActionExclusively, enqueued FIFO. Concurrent native-action requests can no longer overwrite each other's process-global registries or clear them mid-flight. Regression test merged into tests/integration/mcp-dynamic-package-actions.spec.ts: a project extension registers probe-slow (120ms async handler) + probe-fast (10ms); both fired via Promise.all through handleRequest; asserts both return correct markers AND the start/end log shows the two registry cycles are atomic (no interleaving). Verified the test FAILS when the lock is bypassed (log interleaves start:probe-slow,start:probe-fast,...) and passes with it. Full spec 15/15 green, mcp-handshake 14/14 green, tsc --noEmit clean. Residual (not blocking): true request-scoped registry plumbing through every runActive* consumer remains a possible future refactor if intra-server concurrency is ever introduced; serialization fully resolves the race surfaced on PR #150.

value.author: codex-implementation-agent
value.text: Final verification evidence: pnpm build, pnpm typecheck, pnpm contracts:check, pnpm quality:static, pnpm quality:docs-links, pnpm security:scan, pnpm audit --audit-level low, node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage (149 files, 2619 tests, 100% statements/branches/functions/lines), pnpm changelog:pm:check, pm health --check-only --summary, pm validate --check-resolution --check-history-drift, Sentry/telemetry gate (critical=0 high=0 telemetry ok), and isolated temp smoke for command-kit install/echo/list flag/package doctor all passed. Residual validation warning is the known pre-existing 116 older closed items missing resolution fields; history drift is clean.

patch.value: Serialized MCP dynamic extension action activation/run/clear paths to avoid process-global registry races.
Concurrent MCP native-action requests do not race through shared extension registry state.
src/mcp/server.ts uses a serial queue for dynamic extension action mutation and regression tests passed.
MCP dynamic extension action serialization completed.

value.author: codex-implementation-agent
value.text: PR #152 Gemini feedback fix: MCP dynamic extension actions now clear the SDK workspace contracts cache after the serialized activation/run/clear critical section so long-lived MCP servers do not serve stale extension contract data.
